content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a p-code VM and p-code assembler (pasm) for the COR24 24-bit RISC ISA. The VM is a stack-based interpreter written in COR24 assembly. The pasm tool (also COR24 assembly) assembles human-readable .pasm source into bytecode for testing the VM. Pascal compiler is a separate project.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-vm-scaffold [production]: Create pvm.s scaffold: COR24 entry point, VM state struct (pc, esp, csp, fp, ...
  [x] 002-vm-stack-ops [legacy]: Implement push/push_s/dup/drop/swap/over opcode handlers and sys PUTC (opcode 0x60 with id=1). Test by embedding hardcoded bytecode: push_s 72, sys 1, push_s 10, sys 1, halt — should print H-newline to UART.
  [x] 003-vm-arithmetic [legacy]: Implement add/sub/mul/div/mod/neg/and/or/xor/not/shl/shr and eq/ne/lt/le/gt/ge opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded bytecode: push 7, push 6, mul, sys PUTC should output ASCII '*' (42).
  [x] 004-vm-control-flow [legacy]: Implement jmp/jz/jnz opcode handlers. Test with hardcoded countdown loop: push 5, loop: dup, jz done, push_s 1, sub, jmp loop, done: halt.
  [ ] 005-vm-frames [legacy]: Implement call/ret/enter/leave and loadl/storel/loada/storea opcode handlers. Build frame management: push/pop activation records on call stack, argument passing from eval stack to frame. Test with hardcoded bytecode for a factorial function. <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 006-vm-memory [legacy]: Implement loadg/storeg/addrg/addrl and indirect load/store/loadb/storeb. Implement globals segment. Test with hardcoded bytecode: global counter, store/load/print.
  [ ] 007-vm-nonlocal [legacy]: Implement loadn/storen for static link chain traversal (needed for Pascal nested procedures). Test with hardcoded 2-level nested procedure accessing outer local.
  [ ] 008-vm-syscalls [legacy]: Implement remaining sys calls: HALT(0), GETC(2), LED(3), ALLOC(4), FREE(5). Bump allocator for heap. Test: LED toggle, UART echo, heap alloc/write/read.
  [ ] 009-vm-traps [legacy]: Implement trap handling for all trap codes (div-zero, stack overflow/underflow, invalid opcode, invalid address, nil pointer). Print diagnostic to UART on trap. Test each trap condition.
  [ ] 010-pasm-lexer [legacy]: Create pasm.s — p-code assembler in COR24 assembly. Start with lexer: read pasm source from UART, tokenize into token types (mnemonic, number, label, directive, newline, comment, eof). Use a token buffer in memory. Test by feeding simple pasm source and dumping token stream.
  [ ] 011-pasm-parser [legacy]: Implement pasm parser: two-pass assembly. Pass 1: collect labels and procedure addresses, count globals/data sizes. Pass 2: emit bytecode. Handle .const/.global/.data/.proc/.end directives. Test by assembling a minimal program (push 42, halt) and verifying bytecode bytes.
  [ ] 012-pasm-integration [legacy]: Connect pasm output to VM input. Assemble pasm source into bytecode buffer, then execute. Test with hello.pasm (full puts procedure, data string, main calling puts). First end-to-end test.
  [ ] 013-test-suite [legacy]: Create full test suite: tests/t01-arith.pasm through t10-traps.pasm. Create demo.sh test harness with golden output comparison (following tf24a pattern). Create examples/hello.pasm, examples/blink.pasm, examples/echo.pasm.
  [ ] 014-hardening [legacy]: Fix bugs found by test suite. Add stack depth checking. Validate memory bounds. Clean up diagnostic output. Ensure all tests pass. Final documentation update.

=== CURRENT STEP: 005-vm-frames [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Implement call/ret/enter/leave and loadl/storel/loada/storea opcode handlers. Build frame management: push/pop activation records on call stack, argument passing from eval stack to frame. Test with hardcoded bytecode for a factorial function.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.text: Now I need to implement frame management opcodes. Let me read the design spec and current code.

content.content:
1→# pv24a — Design Document
     2→
     3→## 1. VM State
     4→
     5→The p-code VM maintains the following state in a memory struct:
     6→
     7→```
     8→vm_state:
     9→    .word 0     ; pc   — p-code instruction pointer
    10→    .word 0     ; esp  — eval stack pointer (top of eval stack)
    11→    .word 0     ; csp  — call stack pointer (top of call stack)
    12→    .word 0     ; fp   — current frame pointer (call stack)
    13→    .word 0     ; gp   — globals base address
    14→    .word 0     ; hp   — heap pointer (next free)
    15→    .word 0     ; code — base address of p-code segment
    16→    .word 0     ; status — 0=running, 1=halted, 2=trapped
    17→    .word 0     ; trap_code — last trap reason
    18→```
    19→
    20→## 2. P-Code Instruction Set
    21→
    22→### 2.1 Stack / Constants
    23→
    24→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    25→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    26→| `push` imm24 | 4 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push 24-bit immediate |
    27→| `push_s` imm8 | 2 bytes | ( -- n ) | Push sign-extended 8-bit immediate |
    28→| `dup` | 1 byte | ( a -- a a ) | Duplicate top |
    29→| `drop` | 1 byte | ( a -- ) | Discard top |
    30→| `swap` | 1 byte | ( a b -- b a ) | Swap top two |
    31→| `over` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a b a ) | Copy second to top |
    32→
    33→### 2.2 Arithmetic / Logic
    34→
    35→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    36→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    37→| `add` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a+b ) | Add |
    38→| `sub` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a-b ) | Subtract |
    39→| `mul` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a*b ) | Multiply |
    40→| `div` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a/b ) | Divide (trap on b=0) |
    41→| `mod` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a%b ) | Modulo (trap on b=0) |
    42→| `neg` | 1 byte | ( a -- -a ) | Negate |
    43→| `and` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a&b ) | Bitwise AND |
    44→| `or` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a\|b ) | Bitwise OR |
    45→| `xor` | 1 byte | ( a b -- a^b ) | Bitwise XOR |
    46→| `not` | 1 byte | ( a -- ~a ) | Bitwise NOT |
    47→| `shl` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a<<n ) | Shift left |
    48→| `shr` | 1 byte | ( a n -- a>>n ) | Arithmetic shift right |
    49→
    50→### 2.3 Comparison
    51→
    52→All comparisons push 1 (true) or 0 (false):
    53→
    54→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    55→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    56→| `eq` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Equal |
    57→| `ne` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Not equal |
    58→| `lt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less than (signed) |
    59→| `le` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Less or equal (signed) |
    60→| `gt` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater than (signed) |
    61→| `ge` | 1 byte | ( a b -- flag ) | Greater or equal (signed) |
    62→
    63→### 2.4 Control Flow
    64→
    65→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    66→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    67→| `jmp` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( -- ) | Unconditional jump |
    68→| `jz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if zero |
    69→| `jnz` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( flag -- ) | Jump if nonzero |
    70→| `call` addr24 | 4 bytes | ( args... -- ) | Call procedure |
    71→| `ret` nargs8 | 2 bytes | ( [retval] -- [retval] ) | Return, clean nargs |
    72→| `halt` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Stop VM |
    73→| `trap` code8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Trigger trap |
    74→
    75→### 2.5 Local / Global / Nonlocal Access
    76→
    77→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    78→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    79→| `enter` nlocals8 | 2 bytes | ( -- ) | Set up frame, reserve N local slots |
    80→| `leave` | 1 byte | ( -- ) | Tear down frame |
    81→| `loadl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load local at offset |
    82→| `storel` off8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store local at offset |
    83→| `loadg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load global at offset |
    84→| `storeg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store global at offset |
    85→| `addrl` off8 | 2 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of local |
    86→| `addrg` off24 | 4 bytes | ( -- addr ) | Push address of global |
    87→| `loadn` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load nonlocal via static link chain |
    88→| `storen` depth8 off8 | 3 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store nonlocal via static link chain |
    89→
    90→### 2.6 Indirect Memory Access
    91→
    92→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
    93→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
    94→| `load` | 1 byte | ( addr -- val ) | Load word from address |
    95→| `store` | 1 byte | ( val addr -- ) | Store word to address |
    96→| `loadb` | 1 byte | ( addr -- byte ) | Load byte (zero-extended) |
    97→| `storeb` | 1 byte | ( byte addr -- ) | Store byte |
    98→
    99→### 2.7 System / I/O
   100→
   101→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   102→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   103→| `sys` id8 | 2 bytes | (varies) | System call |
   104→
   105→System call IDs:
   106→
   107→| ID | Name | Stack Effect | Description |
   108→|----|------|-------------|-------------|
   109→| 0 | HALT | ( -- ) | Stop execution |
   110→| 1 | PUTC | ( char -- ) | Write byte to UART |
   111→| 2 | GETC | ( -- char ) | Read byte from UART (blocking) |
   112→| 3 | LED | ( state -- ) | Write LED state |
   113→| 4 | ALLOC | ( size -- ptr ) | Bump-allocate heap block |
   114→| 5 | FREE | ( ptr -- ) | Free heap block (no-op in bump mode) |
   115→
   116→### 2.8 Argument Access
   117→
   118→| Opcode | Encoding | Stack Effect | Description |
   119→|--------|----------|-------------|-------------|
   120→| `loada` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( -- val ) | Load argument by index |
   121→| `storea` idx8 | 2 bytes | ( val -- ) | Store argument by index |
   122→
   123→## 3. Call Frame Layout
   124→
   125→When `call` executes, a new frame is pushed onto the call stack:
   126→
   127→```
   128→         ┌──────────────────┐  ← new csp (after enter)
   129→         │ local[N-1]       │  offset = +(N-1)*3 from fp
   130→         │ ...              │
   131→         │ local[0]         │  offset = 0 from fp
   132→fp ───→  ├──────────────────┤
   133→         │ procedure ID     │  offset = -3
   134→         │ static link      │  offset = -6  (lexically enclosing frame)
   135→         │ dynamic link     │  offset = -9  (caller's fp)
   136→         │ return PC        │  offset = -12
   137→         ├──────────────────┤
   138→         │ arg[N-1]         │  offset = -15  (from fp)
   139→         │ ...              │
   140→         │ arg[0]           │  offset = -15 - (N-1)*3
   141→         └──────────────────┘
   142→```
   143→
   144→- **Caller** pushes arguments onto eval stack, then `call addr`
   145→- **call** instruction: pops args from eval stack into call stack frame header,
   146→  pushes return PC, dynamic link, static link, proc ID
   147→- **enter N**: advances csp by N*3 to reserve local slots, sets fp
   148→- **leave**: restores fp to dynamic link, restores csp
   149→- **ret N**: pushes return value (if any) to eval stack, pops frame, cleans N args
   150→
   151→## 4. Calling Convention
   152→
   153→Simple and explicit:
   154→
   155→1. Caller pushes arguments left-to-right onto eval stack
   156→2. Caller executes `call <proc_addr>`
   157→3. Callee executes `enter <nlocals>` (reserves local slots)
   158→4. Callee accesses args via `loada 0`, `loada 1`, etc.
   159→5. Callee accesses locals via `loadl 0`, `loadl 1`, etc.
   160→6. For functions: callee pushes return value onto eval stack
   161→7. Callee executes `ret <nargs>` (cleans args from frame)
   162→8. Caller finds return value (if any) on eval stack
   163→
   164→## 5. P-Code Assembler (pasm) Syntax
   165→
   166→### 5.0 File Extensions
   167→
   168→- `.spc` — p-code assembler source files (input to pasm)
   169→- `.p24` — assembled p-code bytecode files (output of pasm, input to VM)
   170→
   171→### 5.1 Source Format
   172→
   173→```
   174→; Comment (semicolon to end of line)
   175→
   176→.const UART_DATA  -65280      ; named constant
   177→.const UART_STATUS -65279
   178→.const LED_PORT   -65024
   179→
   180→.global counter 1             ; 1 word of global storage
   181→.global buffer 32             ; 32 words
   182→
   183→.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 0   ; "Hello\0" as bytes
   184→
   185→.proc main 0                 ; procedure, 0 locals
   186→    push msg                  ; push address of msg
   187→    call puts
   188→    halt
   189→.end
   190→
   191→.proc puts 1                 ; 1 local (loop pointer)
   192→    loada 0                   ; arg0 = string address
   193→    storel 0                  ; local0 = current ptr
   194→loop:
   195→    loadl 0
   196→    loadb                     ; load byte at ptr
   197→    dup
   198→    jz done                   ; if zero, done
   199→    sys 1                     ; PUTC
   200→    loadl 0
   201→    push 1
   202→    add
   203→    storel 0                  ; ptr++
   204→    jmp loop
   205→done:
   206→    drop                      ; drop the zero
   207→    ret 1                     ; return, clean 1 arg
   208→.end
   209→```
   210→
   211→### 5.2 Directives
   212→
   213→| Directive | Syntax | Description |
   214→|-----------|--------|-------------|
   215→| `.const` | `.const NAME value` | Define named constant |
   216→| `.global` | `.global NAME nwords` | Reserve global storage |
   217→| `.data` | `.data NAME byte, byte, ...` | Define byte data (string/array) |
   218→| `.proc` | `.proc NAME nlocals` | Begin procedure (nlocals = local slot count) |
   219→| `.end` | `.end` | End procedure |
   220→
   221→### 5.3 Labels
   222→
   223→Labels appear on their own line, ending with `:`:
   224→
   225→```
   226→loop:
   227→    ; instructions...
   228→    jmp loop
   229→```
   230→
   231→Labels are local to the enclosing `.proc` / `.end` block.
   232→
   233→### 5.4 Integer Literals
   234→
   235→- Decimal: `42`, `-1`, `0`
   236→- Hex: `0xFF` (if supported) or decimal equivalents
   237→- Named constants: `UART_DATA` (substituted by assembler)
   238→
   239→### 5.5 Instructions
   240→
   241→One instruction per line. Operand follows mnemonic separated by whitespace:
   242→
   243→```
   244→push 42
   245→loadl 0
   246→jz done
   247→call puts
   248→sys 1
   249→ret 0
   250→```
   251→
   252→## 6. Opcode Encoding Table
   253→
   254→Opcodes are assigned sequential byte values:
   255→
   256→| Byte | Mnemonic | Operand |
   257→|------|----------|---------|
   258→| 0x00 | halt | — |
   259→| 0x01 | push | imm24 |
   260→| 0x02 | push_s | imm8 |
   261→| 0x03 | dup | — |
   262→| 0x04 | drop | — |
   263→| 0x05 | swap | — |
   264→| 0x06 | over | — |
   265→| 0x10 | add | — |
   266→| 0x11 | sub | — |
   267→| 0x12 | mul | — |
   268→| 0x13 | div | — |
   269→| 0x14 | mod | — |
   270→| 0x15 | neg | — |
   271→| 0x16 | and | — |
   272→| 0x17 | or | — |
   273→| 0x18 | xor | — |
   274→| 0x19 | not | — |
   275→| 0x1A | shl | — |
   276→| 0x1B | shr | — |
   277→| 0x20 | eq | — |
   278→| 0x21 | ne | — |
   279→| 0x22 | lt | — |
   280→| 0x23 | le | — |
   281→| 0x24 | gt | — |
   282→| 0x25 | ge | — |
   283→| 0x30 | jmp | addr24 |
   284→| 0x31 | jz | addr24 |
   285→| 0x32 | jnz | addr24 |
   286→| 0x33 | call | addr24 |
   287→| 0x34 | ret | nargs8 |
   288→| 0x35 | trap | code8 |
   289→| 0x40 | enter | nlocals8 |
   290→| 0x41 | leave | — |
   291→| 0x42 | loadl | off8 |
   292→| 0x43 | storel | off8 |
   293→| 0x44 | loadg | off24 |
   294→| 0x45 | storeg | off24 |
   295→| 0x46 | addrl | off8 |
   296→| 0x47 | addrg | off24 |
   297→| 0x48 | loada | idx8 |
   298→| 0x49 | storea | idx8 |
   299→| 0x4A | loadn | depth8 off8 |
   300→| 0x4B | storen | depth8 off8 |
   301→| 0x50 | load | — |
   302→| 0x51 | store | — |
   303→| 0x52 | loadb | — |
   304→| 0x53 | storeb | — |
   305→| 0x60 | sys | id8 |
   306→
   307→Reserved ranges 0x70–0xFF for future opcodes (heap, debug, extended ops).
   308→
   309→## 7. Trap Codes
   310→
   311→| Code | Name | Trigger |
   312→|------|------|---------|
   313→| 0 | USER_TRAP | `trap 0` instruction |
   314→| 1 | DIV_ZERO | Division/modulo by zero |
   315→| 2 | STACK_OVERFLOW | Eval or call stack exceeds limit |
   316→| 3 | STACK_UNDERFLOW | Eval stack pop when empty |
   317→| 4 | INVALID_OPCODE | Unknown opcode byte |
   318→| 5 | INVALID_ADDRESS | Memory access out of bounds |
   319→| 6 | NIL_POINTER | Dereference of address 0 |
   320→| 7 | BOUNDS_CHECK | Array index out of bounds |
   321→
   322→On trap: VM sets status=2, trap_code=N, halts. Diagnostic message to UART.
   323→
   324→## 8. Memory Segment Layout
   325→
   326→At VM initialization:
   327→
   328→1. **Code segment** starts at a fixed base address (after VM code)
   329→2. **Constants/data** follows code (emitted by pasm)
   330→3. **Globals** follow data (allocated per `.global` directives)
   331→4. **Call stack** starts after globals, grows upward
   332→5. **Eval stack** starts after call stack region, grows upward
   333→6. **Heap** starts after eval stack region, grows upward
   334→7. Segment base addresses stored in VM state struct
   335→
   336→Sizes are configurable at VM init time. Default allocations:
   337→- Call stack: 4 KB
   338→- Eval stack: 2 KB
   339→- Heap: remaining SRAM up to ~900 KB
   340→
   341→## 9. Implementation Strategy
   342→
   343→### Phase 1: Minimal VM
   344→- Opcode dispatch loop
   345→- push, dup, drop, add, sub, halt
   346→- UART output via sys PUTC
   347→- Hardcoded test bytecode (no pasm yet)
   348→
   349→### Phase 2: Control Flow + Procedures
   350→- jmp, jz, jnz
   351→- call, ret, enter, leave
   352→- loadl, storel, loada, storea
   353→- Frame management
   354→
   355→### Phase 3: Full Instruction Set
   356→- All arithmetic/logic/comparison opcodes
   357→- Globals (loadg, storeg)
   358→- Indirect memory (load, store, loadb, storeb)
   359→- Nonlocal access (loadn, storen)
   360→- All sys calls
   361→
   362→### Phase 4: P-Code Assembler
   363→- Lexer for pasm syntax (UART input)
   364→- Two-pass assembly (collect labels, emit bytes)
   365→- Directive handling (.const, .global, .data, .proc/.end)
   366→- Output bytecode to memory buffer
   367→
   368→### Phase 5: Integration & Test Suite
   369→- pasm assembles test programs
   370→- VM executes assembled bytecode
   371→- Regression test harness (demo.sh)
   372→- Full test coverage per staged plan
   373→
   374→## 10. COR24 Assembly Patterns
   375→
   376→Reference patterns from tf24a for common idioms:
   377→
   378→### Dispatch Table
   379→```asm
   380→; Index into jump table by opcode byte
   381→; r0 = opcode
   382→la r2, dispatch_table
   383→; multiply r0 by 3 (entry size = .word = 3 bytes)
   384→; ... load target address, jmp
   385→```
   386→
   387→### UART Output
   388→```asm
   389→; r0 = character to send
   390→la r2, -65280       ; UART base
   391→tx_wait:
   392→    lb r0, 1(r2)   ; status (sign-extended)
